self_restate_response_verbatim:
Authoring exactly one new EventTrace named test_trace_v32 for the sandbox harness this session — the second M11a-extension-extension Commit C trace, advancing the path toward the combined-N=40 sample target. Restating the four structural constraints in my own paraphrase below, distinct from any prior M11a-extension Commit C1..C10 self-restate response and from M11a-extension-extension C1 self-restate response:

(a) Module-import-resolvable Python literal expression. The trace block evaluates as a frozen-dataclass tree at module import time — every kwarg position binds to a value the interpreter can resolve under the standard `from __future__ import annotations` plus `from dataclasses import dataclass, field` constraints active in sandbox/event_trace.py. No deferred computation, no closure-captured generator that resolves under a different session-state, no monkey-patched constructor that requires a sandbox.world.Event subclass to be registered before `get_trace('test_v32')` returns.

(b) Walrus operator stays out of the trace block. The `:=` token does not appear anywhere in the new test_trace_v32 definition. The walrus failure mode introduced at the test_v11 attempt-1 field-default position does not recur in this trace.

(c) Event.id fields are static literal strings authored once at draft time. No `+` operator concatenating string parts at module-import time, no f-string with embedded arithmetic that evaluates to an integer, no `str(N)` cast at the id field. Each of test_trace_v32's nine Event.id fields is a fixed identifier authored once and verified literal-substring-stable.

(d) Avoid the 226 prior banned event_ids, the 127 banned semantic themes, and the 126 banned keyword tuples enumerated in the prompt above. The avoidance protocol is mechanically stronger than literal-ID set-intersection per the audit-gate locked at M11a-extension D-C1.3 and inherited verbatim into M11a-extension-extension: step 3a does bytewise set-intersection on banned IDs (0/226 collision required); step 3b does per-GT semantic theme hand-audit vs the 127 banned themes (each with distinguishing-qualifier defense); step 3c bytewise tuple intersection (0/126 collision required); step 4 cross-trace literal-ID HALT-gate against the 226-prior-ID union across 26 prior trace definitions including the freshly-landed test_v31 (HALTs the M11a-extension-extension milestone on any collision per §D9 Surface #1); step 5 cross-trace GT-tuple bytewise intersection; step 6 ≥8-word verbatim phrase set-intersection against the ~14000+ prior dev/test 8-grams across content + briefing + intents fields including test_v31. The audit-step-6 8-gram check is the mechanical safety net for the boilerplate-template draft-time author-judgment failure mode (M11a-extension D-C7.attempt1.D / D-C8.attempt1.C / D-C9.attempt1.C / D-C10.attempt1.C + M11a-extension-extension D-C1.attempt1.C boilerplate-paraphrase author-discipline carried forward; tightened at M11a-extension C10 to cover ALL formulaic clauses including launch-window perks, catalog-cadence, customer-service-closings, muting/unsubscribe instructions, next-run-timing closings — discipline applied verbatim at draft time to all four V2 EXISTING NO distractors in test_v32 to preserve the M11a-extension-extension C1 first-run-PASS posture at audit step 6).

boilerplate_paraphrase_discipline_applic.discipline_source: D-C2.attempt1.C inheriting verbatim from M11a-extension D-C7.attempt1.D + D-C8.attempt1.C + D-C9.attempt1.C + D-C10.attempt1.C + M11a-extension-extension D-C1.attempt1.C (TIGHTENED scope: ALL formulaic clauses in distractor content + briefing + intents — closing sentences, opening sentences, template middle sentences, launch-window perks, catalog-cadence announcements, customer-service-closings, muting/unsubscribe instructions, next-run-timing closings)

application_audit_per_distractor.D1_sentry_quarterly_platform_engineering: closing sentence paraphrased per-trace: 'The digest carries the standard Sentry-SaaS tier-utilization summary with no overage projection through the next two quarters, the issue-tag taxonomy compliance report sitting at full coverage across the six tracked projects, and a bottom-of-mail Sentry-platform-status link verifying no platform-side ingestion incidents over the rolling ninety-day window.' — distinct from prior routine-status closings (no 'zero dropped events / zero rotations required / all-pass digest / zero modified resources / zero drift all accounts / all-green zero-flaky-rerun' generic phrasings); product-specific Sentry-SaaS tier-utilization + Sentry-platform-status link routing not present in any prior routine-status distractor.
application_audit_per_distractor.D2_tuesday_late_morning_midweek_pulse_br: middle/closing sentence paraphrased per-trace: 'None of the workspace status indicators are flashing yellow on the morning rollup and the personal-budget tracker confirmed the auto-debit reconciliation against the checking account closed without exceptions for the rolling weekly snapshot window.' — explicitly avoided the M11a-extension generic-daily-briefing template phrasings ('no priority items are flagged from your project list' / 'the personal task tracker rolled over with no overdue rows' / 'project rollup shows no overdue rows or red-flagged blockers' family of phrasings carried-forward from C7..C10 and M11a-extension-extension C1) by introducing the workspace-status-indicator yellow-flashing morning-rollup rephrasing and the personal-budget-tracker auto-debit-reconciliation weekly-snapshot detail (financial-tracker mention NOT in the briefing-template prior phrasings).
application_audit_per_distractor.D3_aws_step_functions_distributed_map_v2: closing sentence paraphrased per-trace: 'Three concurrent secondary launches in the same train round out the train summary; the engineering architecture deep-dive is scheduled on the AWS Containers and Serverless YouTube channel later this week and the matching CDK construct lands in the next aws-cdk minor-version drop.' — explicitly avoided the M11a-extension feature-announcement closing-template phrasings ('each detailed on the dedicated changelog page' from C10 Figma + 'walkthrough on the engineering blog linked under the changelog entry' from C10 Figma + 'engineering deep-dive on the team-pool fairness algorithm running on the Calendly product blog from this morning' from M11a-extension-extension C1) by domain-specific routing to AWS Containers and Serverless YouTube channel + aws-cdk minor-version drop CDK construct (distinct delivery-channels not present in prior feature-announcement closings).
application_audit_per_distractor.D4_letterboxd_friends_activity_weekly_di: closing sentence paraphrased per-trace: 'The mail carries a follow-suggestion list pre-screened against the existing follow graph, an opt-out toggle for the friends-activity category that survives the cadence rotation, and a bottom-of-mail link routing to the friends-circle settings panel on the Letterboxd profile-preferences page where the notification-cadence controls live and the per-friend visibility toggles are surfaced for granular adjustment.' — explicitly avoided the M11a-extension social-channel-invite closing templates ('one-tap follow buttons inline / opt-out link for the curated-circle category / a contextual summary card explaining why each suggested account surfaced this round' from M11a-extension-extension C1 Threads-by-Meta + 'pre-filtered against the existing follow set and the muted-keyword list already configured on the account' from M11a-extension-extension C1 closing); 'follow-suggestion list pre-screened against the existing follow graph' (substitutes 'pre-screened' for 'pre-filtered') + 'opt-out toggle' (substitutes 'toggle' for 'link') + 'survives the cadence rotation' (Letterboxd-specific cadence framing) + 'friends-circle settings panel' (Letterboxd-specific UI routing) + 'per-friend visibility toggles' (Letterboxd-specific granular control surface).

TIGHTENED draft-time discipline effective at preventing the boilerplate-template C7-attempt-1 + C9-attempt-1 convergence rejection modes at the distractor level; the one inadvertent 8-gram landing was in intent #1 (a paraphrase of test_v31 intent #1's DKA-emergency 911-pathway phrasing) NOT in a distractor — distractor-level discipline held through the audit-gate first run; in-session step-6 revision targeted the inadvertent intent-level cross-trace overlap exclusively.
